It integrates services such as OneLake, Data Factory, Synapse, and Power BI to enable seamless data workflows, governance, and analytics. Power BI is a business analytics service that enables users to visualize data, share insights, and make informed decisions. It connects to multiple data sources, including Microsoft Fabric, and supports advanced modeling and reporting. Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS adopt the following data workflow considerations to ensure robust data ingestion, transformation, machine learning, and reporting capabilities. | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering| | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering| | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering| | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering| | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following design decisions for ETL, machine learning, and reporting workflows. UMS shall implement these workflows as illustrated in the referenced diagrams. ![](images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: End-to-end data workflow architecture. ![](images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: AutoML and manual ML workflow integration. | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01| Adopt Data Factory pipelines for ingestion from Microsoft SQL Server into Bronze Lakehouse. | Enfrasys Solutions to configure pipelines; UMS to provide source credentials. | | DD02| Implement Bronze, Silver, and Gold Lakehouse layers for data engineering and transformation. | Enfrasys Solutions to design Lakehouse schema; UMS to validate data requirements. | | DD03| Use Dataflow Gen2, Notebooks, and Visual Query for cleansing and transformation in Silver Lakehouse. | Enfrasys Solutions to develop workflows; UMS to supply transformation logic. | | DD04| Store ML results (AutoML and manual) in Gold Lakehouse for unified reporting. | Enfrasys Solutions to integrate ML outputs; UMS to review scoring outputs. | | DD05| Connect Power BI Semantic Model to Gold Lakehouse via OneLake for real-time reporting. | Enfrasys Solutions to configure Semantic Model; UMS to design report layouts. | | DD06| Register trained ML models in Fabric Model Registry for lifecycle management and reuse. | Enfrasys Solutions to set up registry; UMS to manage model versions. | | DD07| Apply scoring notebooks for new/unseen data, storing results in Gold Lakehouse. | Enfrasys Solutions to automate scoring; UMS to monitor performance metrics. | ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions ### Conditional Access Microsoft Entra ID Conditional Access provides automated access control decisions for cloud apps based on conditions such as user, location, and device state. It is essential for securing access to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. ![](images\slide_006_img_001.png) Figure 3: Conditional Access enforcement for external users. ![](images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 4: Conditional Access workflow for Microsoft Fabric. | No. | Policy | Decision | |------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01 | Configure Policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage | Enfrasys Solutions to design policy; UMS to review affected users. | | DD02 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy | Enfrasys Solutions to update policy; UMS to validate user list. | | DD03 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts | Enfrasys Solutions to enforce MFA; UMS to communicate MFA requirements. | | DD04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment | Enfrasys Solutions to validate access; UMS to monitor user experience. | ### Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Due to licensing constraints, Conditional Access cannot be fully implemented for UMS at this stage. Enfrasys Solutions recommends enforcing MFA at the tenant level to secure access to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI until Conditional Access licensing is acquired. | No. | Policy | Decision | |------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD05 | Enforce MFA for all UMS users | Enfrasys Solutions to configure MFA; UMS to support user onboarding. | ### External User Access External users require secure access to UMS Fabric resources. Enfrasys Solutions recommends that UMS review external user requirements and enforce MFA for all guest accounts. | No. | Policy | Decision | |------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD06 | Enforce MFA for external users accessing UMS Fabric | Enfrasys Solutions to configure guest MFA; UMS to approve external user list. | ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size UMS shall implement a dedicated VM for the Data Gateway to facilitate secure and reliable connectivity between on-premises data sources and Microsoft Fabric/Power BI. | Component | Specification | |------------------|-------------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | Enfrasys Solutions will provision and configure the VM; UMS will provide network access and monitor performance. ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### Management Groups Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS establish a logical hierarchy using Azure Management Groups to organize subscriptions, resource groups, and resources for Microsoft Fabric and Power BI workloads. This structure supports policy enforcement, cost management, and access control. - Root Management Group: UMS-Tenant - Sub-management Group: UMS-Fabric - Resource Group: Fabric-RG - Resources: Fabric Capacity, Workspaces, Lakehouses, Dataflows, Pipelines, Reports UMS is responsible for maintaining the management group hierarchy; Enfrasys Solutions will assist in initial setup and policy assignment. ### Naming and Tagging #### Azure Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |-------------------------|-------------|-------------------------| | Resource Group | RG | Fabric-RG | | Virtual Machine | VM | DataGateway-VM | | Storage Account | SA | umsdatastore01 | | Network Security Group | NSG | Fabric-NSG | #### Fabric Naming Convention | No. | Item in Fabric | Naming Convention | |-----|------------------|----------------------------------------| | 1 | Fabric Capacity | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | 2 | Workspace | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | 3 | Lakehouse | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK
StudentAffairs_Silver_LK | | 4 | Dataflow | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF
StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF | | 5 | Semantic Model | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | 6 | Pipeline | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL | | 7 | Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report| #### Acronyms | Resource | Abbreviation | |-------------------------|-------------| | Lakehouse | LK | | Dataflow | DF | | Semantic Model | SM | | Pipeline | PL | | Workspace | WS | UMS shall apply these naming conventions and tagging standards to all resources. Enfrasys Solutions will validate compliance during rollout. ## 6.6 Governance Consideration ### Governance Disciplines UMS must adopt governance disciplines to ensure cost management, resource optimization, and compliance. Enfrasys Solutions will provide guidance and periodic reviews. | Cost Item | Owner | Review Frequency | |-------------------|--------------------|-------------------------| | Fabric Capacity | UMS IT | Monthly | | Workspace Usage | UMS IT | Quarterly | | VM Consumption | UMS IT | Monthly | | Data Storage | UMS IT | Quarterly | | Power BI License | UMS Finance | Annually | ### Violation Triggers and Actions | Trigger | Action | Owner | |---------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------|--------------------| | Excessive Fabric Capacity consumption | Notify, review, optimize workload | UMS IT | | Unauthorized workspace creation | Audit, restrict, enforce policy | Enfrasys Solutions | | VM resource over-utilization | Scale up/down, optimize configuration | UMS IT | | Data storage threshold exceeded | Alert, archive, expand storage | UMS IT | | License compliance breach | Review, purchase additional licenses | UMS Finance | Enfrasys Solutions will provide automated monitoring and reporting; UMS is responsible for remediation actions. --- Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) strictly adhere to the above design, naming conventions, access policies, and governance practices to ensure a secure, scalable, and compliant Microsoft Fabric and Power BI environment. Ownership of implementation tasks is clearly assigned to Enfrasys Solutions and UMS as specified in each section. # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section defines the security architecture for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), focusing on Azure Network Security Group (NSG) rules and encryption strategies for all critical data flows. ## 7.1 Azure NSG Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a fundamental security feature within Microsoft Azure, enabling administrators to control inbound and outbound traffic to Azure resources at both subnet and VM levels. NSGs contain rules that specify allowed or denied traffic based on source, destination, port, and protocol. Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) implement NSG configurations as outlined below to secure the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM and ensure compliant connectivity with Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and associated Microsoft cloud services. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the design and configuration of NSG rules; UMS is responsible for ongoing compliance and monitoring. ### Outbound NSG Rules | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |--------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services| 130 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication| 140 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ### Inbound NSG Rules No inbound rules are required for OPDG VM connectivity to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI, as all communication is initiated outbound. Enfrasys Solutions will ensure that only essential outbound rules are configured, minimizing the attack surface. ## 7.2 Encryption Design Encryption is a critical component in safeguarding data as it moves between UMS’s infrastructure and Microsoft cloud services. Enfrasys Solutions will architect encryption mechanisms for both data at rest and data in transit, ensuring compliance with Microsoft security standards. ### Encryption at Rest - **Microsoft Fabric, OneLake, Power BI:** All data stored within Microsoft Fabric, OneLake, and Power BI is encrypted at rest using Microsoft-managed keys (AES-256) as per Microsoft’s default security posture. Customers may opt for customer-managed keys for additional control, subject to licensing and configuration. - **OPDG VM:** Data stored locally on the OPDG VM is encrypted using Azure Disk Encryption, leveraging BitLocker (Windows) or DM-Crypt (Linux). ### Encryption in Transit - **OPDG to Microsoft Fabric/Power BI:** All outbound traffic from OPDG VM to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and other Microsoft cloud endpoints is encrypted using SSL/TLS (HTTPS, TCP port 443). - **User Access via Entra ID:** Authentication and access control traffic between UMS users and Microsoft Fabric is secured using TLS 1.2/1.3, enforced by Microsoft Entra ID. - **Conditional Access (MFA):** Multi-factor authentication is enforced at the tenant level, ensuring secure user verification and reducing unauthorized access risks. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the security design and implementation of encryption mechanisms. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for compliance monitoring and periodic review of encryption policies. ![](images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 1: Conditional access workflow for Microsoft Fabric authentication. ## 7.3 Security Task Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|--------------| | NSG Rule Design & Configuration| Define and implement outbound NSG rules for OPDG VM | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Encryption Policy Design | Architect encryption at rest and in transit for all data flows | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Compliance Monitoring | Review and monitor NSG and encryption compliance | Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) | Ongoing | | MFA Enforcement | Enforce tenant-level Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) | Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) | Completed | ## 7.4 Access Decisions | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|----------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Conditional Access for UMS Tenant | Not eligible for Conditional Access; MFA enforced at tenant level | | 2 | Conditional Access for External Users | External users subject to MFA and basic access policies | | 3 | Private Link for Fabric | Deferred due to current limitations; revisit in future expansion | | 4 | Licensing for Conditional Access | Additional licensing (Entra ID P1/P2) required for advanced policies; not implemented in current phase | ![](images\slide_006_img_001.png) Figure 2: Access control architecture for UMS Fabric resources. --- Enfrasys Solutions will deliver the initial security configuration and documentation. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall implement ongoing monitoring, user training, and compliance activities as part of their operational responsibilities. # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the formal deployment and migration methodology recommended by Enfrasys Solutions for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), including preparatory actions, pilot rollout, production migration, and post-migration support. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup Enfrasys Solutions recommends a structured pre-migration phase to ensure readiness and minimize risk. All preparatory tasks are detailed below, with clear ownership assignment. | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|-----------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|-----------------|-------------|---------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | Enfrasys Solutions shall provide technical guidance and conduct environment setup, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall confirm source data and participate in gateway installation. ## 8.2 Pilot Deployment Enfrasys Solutions recommends a pilot deployment to validate the migration approach and ensure system stability prior to full production rollout. ### Pilot Steps - Deploy Microsoft Fabric environment in a controlled test workspace. - Ingest sample data from the staging area database via On-Premises Data Gateway. - Validate connectivity, data refresh, and performance. - Conduct user acceptance testing (UAT) with designated UMS stakeholders. ### Acceptance Criteria - Successful data ingestion and refresh cycles. - No critical errors in connectivity or transformation. - UMS confirms pilot workspace meets functional and security requirements. - Signed pilot acceptance by UMS. ## 8.3 Production Deployment Production deployment shall be executed in structured waves with defined timelines. Enfrasys Solutions will lead technical execution; Universiti Malaysia Sabah will provide data access and user validation. ### Migration Timeline Table | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |--------------|--------------------------------------------------------|-----------------|------------| | D-7 | Final production readiness review, backup of source DB | Enfrasys, UMS | Pending | | D-3 | Finalize user access lists, confirm workspace roles | UMS | Pending | | D0 | Cutover: Data migration, activation of Fabric workspace | Enfrasys | Pending | | D0 | Validation: Data integrity checks, workspace access | Enfrasys, UMS | Pending | | D+1 | Post-cutover support, monitor data refresh, resolve issues | Enfrasys | Pending | | D+3 | User feedback collection, minor adjustments | UMS, Enfrasys | Pending | Enfrasys Solutions will coordinate all technical cutover activities, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah will validate access and provide feedback. ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support Post-migration support ensures operational stability and knowledge transfer. Responsibilities are clearly assigned. ### Support & Handover Activities | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |---------------------|---------------------------------------------------|-----------------|------------| | Knowledge Transfer | Conduct admin and user training sessions | Enfrasys | Pending | | Documentation | Deliver migration and configuration documentation | Enfrasys | Pending | | Monitoring Setup | Configure monitoring and alerting for Fabric | Enfrasys | Pending | | Issue Resolution | Address post-migration incidents | Enfrasys | Pending | | Acceptance | Formal handover and sign-off by UMS | UMS | Pending | Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall participate in training, review documentation, and provide formal acceptance. Enfrasys Solutions will deliver all technical and operational support during the post-migration phase. # 9.0 Appendix ## 9.1 Appendix 1 Computing ### Introduction/Prerequisites [Azure Compute Overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/) [Azure VM Deployment Prerequisites](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/overview) ### Limits and Boundaries [Azure VM Size and Quota Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes) [Azure Subscription Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits) ### Others [Azure Reserved Instances](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/reservations/save-compute-costs-reserved-vm-instances) [Azure Hybrid Benefit](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/hybrid-use-benefit-licensing) ## The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving Azure components and user access management. 

1. **Fabric-RG**: This represents a Resource Group in Azure, which is a container that holds related resources for an Azure solution. It is used to manage and organize resources effectively.

2. **UMS Fabric**: This component likely refers to a service or application running within the Azure environment, possibly related to user management or a specific business application.

3. **Conditional Access**: This is a security feature in Azure Active Directory that provides automated access control decisions for accessing cloud apps based on conditions. It ensures that only authorized users can access certain resources under specific conditions.

4. **External User**: Represents users outside the organization who need access to the UMS Fabric. These users are subject to the Conditional Access policies to ensure secure and compliant access.

The data flow indicates that external users access the UMS Fabric through a Conditional Access policy, ensuring secure and controlled access to the resources within the Fabric-RG. The arrow signifies the direction of access and interaction between the external user and the UMS Fabric, mediated by the Conditional Access policies.

The image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating the authentication and access control process for Microsoft Fabric using Entra conditional access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**: The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**: The authentication request is evaluated against Entra conditional access policies.

3. **Signal Collection**: Signals are collected, which include examples such as users and groups, and network location.

4. **Decision Making**: Based on the collected signals, a decision is made regarding the access request.

5. **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, determining whether access is granted or denied.

6. **Access to Microsoft Fabric**: If access is granted, the user can interact with Microsoft Fabric and its components, including OneLake and other services depicted by various icons.

The diagram outlines a secure and conditional access workflow, ensuring that only authorized users can access Microsoft Fabric based on predefined policies.

The image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating a data processing workflow within an Azure environment, specifically using UMS Fabric and OneLake.

1. **Data Ingestion**: 
   - The process begins with data ingestion from a source labeled "UMS – Staging Area – Microsoft SQL Server 2017 – Data Warehouse."
   - Data is ingested using Azure Data Factory.

2. **Data Processing Stages**:
   - **Bronze Layer**: The ingested data is first stored in "XX_Bronze_LK Tables." This layer typically contains raw data.
   - **Silver Layer**: Data is then processed and moved to "XX_Silver_LK Tables," where it is likely cleaned and transformed for further analysis.
   - **Gold Layer**: The final processed data is stored in "XX_Gold_LK Tables," optimized for reporting and analytics.

3. **Data Science and Transformation**:
   - Between the Silver and Gold layers, there is a Data Science component, indicating that machine learning or advanced analytics might be applied to the data.
   - Another instance of Data Factory is used here, suggesting additional data transformation or orchestration tasks.

4. **Identity Management**:
   - An "Identity_SM" component is present, which may handle security, access control, or identity management for the data.

5. **Data Storage and Access**:
   - The entire process is encapsulated within "UMS Fabric," indicating a structured data management framework.
   - Data is stored in "OneLake," a unified data lake solution, facilitating seamless data access and integration.

6. **Data Visualization**:
   - The processed data in the Gold layer is connected to Power BI via OneLake, enabling data visualization and business intelligence reporting.

This architecture supports a robust data processing pipeline, leveraging Azure services for efficient data management, transformation, and analytics.

The diagram illustrates a data flow architecture involving three main components:

1. **UMS – Staging_Area – Microsoft SQL Server 2017 – Data Warehouse**: This component represents a data warehouse using Microsoft SQL Server 2017. It serves as the staging area for data storage and processing.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**: This component acts as a bridge between the on-premises data warehouse and the cloud-based UMS Fabric. It facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports, ensuring data security and compliance.

3. **UMS Fabric**: This is the cloud-based component that interacts with the on-premises data through the OPDG. It receives data via an outbound secure connection, which enhances security by eliminating the need for open inbound ports.

The data flow begins at the UMS Staging_Area, where data is stored and processed. It is then securely transmitted to the UMS Fabric through the On-Premise Data Gateway, which manages the connection and ensures secure data transfer.

The diagram illustrates a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure environment, integrating UMS Fabric and OneLake.

1. **Data Source**: 
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Acts as the initial data source, feeding into two potential machine learning paths.

2. **Machine Learning Paths**:
   - **AutoML**:
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Data from XX_Silver_LK Tables is processed through an automated machine learning experiment.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: The results are scored using a notebook, indicating a feedback loop for model evaluation and refinement.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modelling (Using Notebook)**: Data is manually modeled using a notebook, allowing for custom model development and experimentation.

3. **Data Storage and Processing**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: Serves as the refined data storage, receiving processed data from both AutoML and Manual Machine Learning paths.
   - **Identity_SM**: Likely a service or module for identity management or further data processing, connected to XX_Gold_LK Tables.

4. **Data Visualization and Reporting**:
   - **Power BI**: Connected via OneLake to XX_Gold_LK Tables, enabling data visualization and reporting.

The workflow demonstrates a flexible approach to machine learning, offering both automated and manual options, with a focus on data refinement and visualization.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the Power BI & Microsoft Fabric implementation project for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). ## 2.1 Project Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is undertaking a strategic initiative to modernize its data analytics and business intelligence capabilities through the adoption of Microsoft Power BI and Microsoft Fabric. The project scope encompasses designing a robust data workflow that spans data ingestion, transformation, and visualization, leveraging Microsoft Fabric’s integrated platform. Key objectives include: - Establishing a dedicated Fabric workspace for UMS. - Developing an end-to-end ETL pipeline utilizing Fabric Data Factory for seamless data integration and orchestration. - Implementing advanced analytics and big data processing via Fabric Synapse. - Enabling machine learning processes for business-driven insights. - Integrating Power BI for comprehensive, business-aligned analytics and reporting. This initiative is aligned with UMS’s cloud adoption strategy and aims to empower data-driven decision-making across the organization. ## 2.2 Document Purpose The purpose of this Solution Design Document is to provide a detailed, authoritative blueprint for the technical design, implementation, and integration of Microsoft Power BI and Fabric within UMS. It serves as the foundation for technical alignment between Universiti Malaysia Sabah and Enfrasys Solutions, and will be used for formal sign-off prior to project execution. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for drafting this document; UMS is responsible for review and approval. ## 2.3 Document Audience The intended audience for this Solution Design Document includes: - UMS IT Department - UMS Business Stakeholders - Enfrasys Solutions Project Team Enfrasys Solutions is accountable for preparing and maintaining the document. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall review, provide feedback, and approve the final version as part of the project governance process. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric and Power BI Overview This section provides formal definitions of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID, outlining their roles and integration in Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) data workflow solution. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for ensuring content accuracy. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Definition Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform designed to unify data engineering, data science, data integration, and business intelligence within a single cloud-based environment. It enables organizations to manage the entire data lifecycle—from ingestion to transformation, storage, and visualization—using a suite of interconnected services. The core services within Microsoft Fabric include: - **Data Factory:** Facilitates data integration and orchestration, allowing users to build, schedule, and manage Extract, Transform, Load (ETL) pipelines for diverse data sources. - **Synapse:** Provides advanced analytics and big data processing capabilities, supporting real-time and batch analytics on large datasets. - **Data Science:** Offers tools and environments for developing, training, and deploying machine learning models to extract predictive insights from data. - **OneLake:** Serves as a unified data lake storage layer, ensuring seamless data access and sharing across all Fabric services. Enfrasys Solutions recommends Universiti Malaysia Sabah implement Microsoft Fabric to streamline data workflows, enhance analytics capabilities, and support scalable machine learning processes. ## 3.2 Power BI Definition and Integration Power BI is a business analytics service by Microsoft that enables users to visualize data, share insights, and drive informed decision-making through interactive dashboards and reports. Power BI integrates natively with Microsoft Fabric, allowing seamless access to datasets stored in OneLake, and leveraging Fabric’s ETL and analytics pipelines for real-time and historical data visualization. This integration empowers Universiti Malaysia Sabah to: - Visualize transformed and cleansed data directly from Fabric workspaces. - Enable self-service analytics for business users. - Maintain a consistent, secure, and scalable analytics environment. Enfrasys Solutions will ensure that Power BI is tightly integrated with Fabric, supporting UMS’s business-aligned analytics requirements. ## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID Definition Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. It provides authentication and authorization mechanisms for users, applications, and resources across Microsoft 365, Azure, and Microsoft Fabric environments. Key functions include: - Secure user authentication for accessing Fabric and Power BI resources. - Role-based access control (RBAC) to ensure only authorized personnel can view or manage data and analytics assets. - Centralized identity management for compliance and auditability. Enfrasys Solutions assigns responsibility for configuring and maintaining Entra ID to ensure Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s data environment remains secure and compliant. # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network topology, connectivity, and security controls for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) Microsoft Fabric deployment, including the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) architecture. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a single SaaS solution. It enables organizations to securely connect on-premises data sources to cloud analytics workloads. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall implement a hybrid network topology, connecting on-premises data assets to Microsoft Fabric via the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG). The OPDG acts as a secure bridge, enabling outbound-only communication from the UMS data warehouse to Fabric and related Microsoft cloud services. All connections are established outbound, eliminating the need for inbound firewall rules and minimizing exposure. ![](images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 1: OPDG data flow architecture. ## 4.2 Fabric Data Gateway (OPDG) Architecture The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a Microsoft-provided agent that facilitates secure data transfer between on-premises sources and cloud services such as Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. It leverages outbound HTTPS connections, ensuring compliance with Azure networking best practices and organizational security requirements. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for deploying and configuring the OPDG agent within a dedicated virtual machine. The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving Azure components and user access management. 

1. **Fabric-RG**: This represents a Resource Group in Azure, which is a container that holds related resources for an Azure solution. It is used to manage and organize resources effectively.

2. **UMS Fabric**: This component likely represents a service or application layer within the Azure environment, possibly related to user management or a specific application fabric.

3. **Conditional Access**: This is a security feature in Azure Active Directory that provides automated access control decisions for accessing cloud apps based on conditions. It is used to enforce security policies.

4. **External User**: Represents users outside the organization who need access to the system. These users are subject to the conditional access policies defined.

**Data Flow and Connections**:
- The arrow indicates a data flow or access request from the External User to the UMS Fabric.
- The Conditional Access component acts as a gatekeeper, evaluating the access request based on predefined security policies before allowing interaction with the UMS Fabric.
- The entire setup is organized within the Fabric-RG, ensuring that all resources are managed under a single resource group for streamlined administration and policy enforcement.

The diagram illustrates an authentication and access control workflow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra conditional access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**: The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**: The authentication request is evaluated against Entra conditional access policies.

3. **Signal Collection**: Signals are gathered, which include user and group information, as well as network location data.

4. **Decision Making**: Based on the collected signals, a decision is made regarding the access request.

5. **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, determining whether the user is granted or denied access.

6. **Access to Microsoft Fabric**: If access is granted, the user can interact with Microsoft Fabric and its components, including OneLake and various services represented by icons.

This workflow ensures secure and conditional access to Microsoft Fabric resources based on predefined policies.

The image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating a data processing workflow within an Azure environment, specifically using UMS Fabric and OneLake.

1. **Data Ingestion**:
   - **Source**: Data originates from the "UMS – Staging Area – Microsoft SQL Server 2017 – Data Warehouse."
   - **Process**: Data is ingested using Azure Data Factory.

2. **Data Processing Layers**:
   - **Bronze Layer**: Data is first stored in "XX_Bronze_LK Tables," representing raw or minimally processed data.
   - **Silver Layer**: Data is then transformed and stored in "XX_Silver_LK Tables," indicating a more refined dataset.
   - **Gold Layer**: Final transformations occur, and data is stored in "XX_Gold_LK Tables," ready for analysis and reporting.

3. **Data Science and Transformation**:
   - **Data Factory**: Used for orchestrating data movement and transformation.
   - **Data Science**: Involves advanced analytics and machine learning processes, likely using Azure Machine Learning or similar services.
   - **Identity_SM**: Represents a component for identity management or security, ensuring data integrity and access control.

4. **Data Storage and Access**:
   - **OneLake**: Acts as a unified data lake, facilitating seamless data storage and access across different layers.
   - **Power BI**: The final data is connected via OneLake to Power BI for visualization and business intelligence reporting.

This architecture supports a structured data processing pipeline, enabling efficient data transformation, storage, and analysis.

The diagram illustrates a data flow architecture involving three main components:

1. **UMS – Staging_Area – Microsoft SQL Server 2017 – Data Warehouse**: This component represents a data warehouse using Microsoft SQL Server 2017. It serves as the staging area for data storage and processing.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**: This component acts as a bridge between the on-premises data warehouse and the cloud service. It facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports, ensuring data security and compliance.

3. **UMS Fabric**: This component represents a cloud-based service or platform that interacts with the on-premises data through the OPDG. The connection is outbound and secure, indicating that data is pushed from the on-premises environment to the cloud service.

The data flow is as follows:
- Data is stored and managed in the on-premises SQL Server 2017 data warehouse.
- The On-Premise Data Gateway enables secure, outbound data transfer from the data warehouse to the UMS Fabric.
- The UMS Fabric receives and processes the data, leveraging cloud capabilities for further analysis or integration.

The diagram illustrates a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure environment, specifically utilizing UMS Fabric and OneLake.

1. **Data Source**: 
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Acts as the initial data source, providing input data for machine learning processes.

2. **Machine Learning Processes**:
   - **AutoML**: 
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Conducts automated machine learning experiments on the data from XX_Silver_LK Tables.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: Scores the results of the AutoML experiments, likely using a Jupyter Notebook for analysis and visualization.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modelling (Using Notebook)**: Involves manual model development and training using a notebook, providing an alternative to AutoML.

3. **Data Transformation and Storage**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: Stores the processed and refined data, which is the output of either the AutoML or Manual Machine Learning processes.
   - **Identity_SM**: Likely a service or module for managing identities or metadata associated with the data in XX_Gold_LK Tables.

4. **Data Visualization and Reporting**:
   - **Power BI**: Connects to the XX_Gold_LK Tables via OneLake for data visualization and reporting, enabling insights and decision-making.

5. **Integration**:
   - **UMS Fabric**: Provides the overarching framework or infrastructure for managing the data flow and machine learning processes.
   - **OneLake**: Facilitates data storage and connectivity, ensuring seamless integration between data sources and Power BI.

The workflow supports both automated and manual machine learning approaches, allowing flexibility in model development and deployment.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) Power BI & Microsoft Fabric solution design, its purpose, and its intended audience. ## 2.1 Project Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) has initiated a project to modernize its data analytics capabilities by leveraging Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. The business context centers on enabling data-driven decision-making through robust data workflows, advanced analytics, and machine learning. The project scope encompasses the design and implementation of a comprehensive data workflow, including data ingestion, transformation, and visualization within Microsoft Fabric. Key objectives include: - Establishing a dedicated Microsoft Fabric workspace for UMS. - Developing end-to-end ETL pipelines to streamline data integration and orchestration. - Conducting machine learning processes to support business analytics. - Integrating Power BI for visualization and actionable insights. - Aligning UMS’s cloud adoption strategy with the latest Microsoft Fabric services, specifically Fabric Data Factory, Fabric Synapse, and Data Science components. ## 2.2 Document Purpose The Solution Design Document (SDD) serves as the authoritative blueprint for the implementation and governance of the UMS Power BI & Microsoft Fabric solution. Its intent is to: - Detail the technical architecture, workflow design, and integration points. - Provide guidance and standards for deployment, operational management, and security. - Facilitate clear communication between Universiti Malaysia Sabah and Enfrasys Solutions regarding responsibilities, timelines, and deliverables. - Act as the reference for future audits, enhancements, and operational adoption. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the authorship, technical accuracy, and periodic review of this SDD. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for formal sign-off and operational adoption of the recommendations and designs contained herein. ## 2.3 Document Audience The intended audience for this Solution Design Document includes: - UMS IT Department (Infrastructure, Security, Operations) - UMS Analytics Stakeholders (Business Intelligence, Data Science, Reporting) - Enfrasys Solutions Project Team (Architects, Engineers, Consultants) - UMS Executive Sponsors and Governance Committee Enfrasys Solutions will author, maintain, and review the SDD. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall be responsible for sign-off and ensuring operational adoption in accordance with the design recommendations. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Entra ID Overview This section provides formal definitions and roles of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID within the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) solution architecture. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, data integration, and business intelligence under a single SaaS offering. Fabric delivers a comprehensive suite of services, including Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing, Data Science for machine learning applications, and OneLake for unified data storage. Fabric’s core services are defined as follows: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |----|-------------|---------------| | 1 | Data Factory: Enables scalable data ingestion, transformation, and orchestration workflows. | ETL/Data Integration | | 2 | Synapse: Provides analytics, big data processing, and SQL-based querying capabilities. | Data Analytics | | 3 | Data Science: Supports AutoML, machine learning model training, and deployment. | Machine Learning | | 4 | OneLake: Centralized, secure data lake storage for all Fabric workloads. | Data Storage | Within the UMS solution, Microsoft Fabric will serve as the foundational platform for data ingestion, transformation, analytics, and machine learning, supporting end-to-end business use cases and aligning with UMS’s cloud adoption strategy. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for technology selection, integration architecture, and Fabric workspace configuration. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall be responsible for user enablement, workspace management, and license procurement. ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is Microsoft’s enterprise-grade business intelligence and data visualization platform. It enables organizations to transform raw data into actionable insights through interactive dashboards, reports, and analytics. Power BI integrates natively with Microsoft Fabric, allowing seamless access to data stored in OneLake and processed via Synapse and Data Factory. Power BI’s analytics and visualization capabilities include: | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-----------------|----------| | 1 | Integration with Fabric | Power BI will connect directly to Fabric workspaces for real-time analytics | | 2 | Visualization | Power BI will be used for business-aligned dashboards and reports | | 3 | Security | Access to Power BI artifacts will be governed by Entra ID conditional access policies | Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing Power BI integration and configuring secure access. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall enable users, assign roles, and manage licensing for Power BI. ## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. Entra ID provides secure authentication, user identity management, and conditional access controls for cloud and SaaS applications, including Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. Key capabilities of Entra ID include: | Capability | Description | |------------|-------------| | Authentication | Provides secure sign-in for users and applications | | Identity Management | Manages user accounts, groups, and roles | | Conditional Access | Enforces access policies based on user, device, and location | | Integration | Enables SSO and role-based access across Microsoft Fabric and Power BI | Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing and implementing Entra ID integration, including conditional access policies and role mapping. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall manage user provisioning, role assignment, and license administration. # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network architecture and connectivity strategy for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), including the rationale for design choices and gateway deployment. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Enfrasys Solutions recommends a hybrid network topology that securely connects UMS’s on-premises environment to Microsoft Fabric and related Microsoft cloud services. The architecture leverages outbound connectivity from the on-premises network to Azure, utilizing the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) for secure data transfer. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall implement outbound-only connectivity from its data center to Azure, avoiding inbound connections to minimize security risks. Private Link is not adopted at this stage due to licensing constraints, eligibility limitations, and the complexity of configuration for UMS’s current Microsoft Entra ID tenant. Enfrasys Solutions will revisit Private Link integration in future phases if UMS upgrades its tenant or acquires suitable licenses. The network design ensures that all data transfers and authentication requests are performed over encrypted channels (HTTPS), with outbound firewall rules enforced to restrict traffic to essential Microsoft endpoints. ## 4.2 Fabric Data Gateway Architecture Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that provides data integration, transformation, and visualization capabilities across Microsoft services such as Power BI, Azure Data Explorer, and OneLake. The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a Microsoft component that enables secure, outbound connectivity between on-premises data sources and Microsoft Fabric, allowing cloud services to access local data without exposing inbound ports. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing the network architecture and deploying the OPDG virtual machine. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for preparing on-premises prerequisites, including the provisioning of the VM, installation of the required OS and .NET Framework, and configuring local firewall policies. ![](images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 1: OPDG architecture connecting on-premises SQL Server to Microsoft Fabric. ### VM Specifications for OPDG | Component | Specification | |-------------------|-----------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ### Outbound Connectivity Rules | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |-------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ## 4.3 Responsibility Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-----------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------|-------------| | OPDG VM Provisioning | Prepare VM with required OS, .NET, CPU, and RAM | Universiti Malaysia Sabah| Pending | | Network Architecture Design | Define topology, configure outbound rules, and document connectivity | Enfrasys Solutions | In Progress | | OPDG Deployment | Install gateway agent, validate connectivity to Microsoft Fabric | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Firewall Configuration | Implement outbound NSG/firewall rules as per table above | Universiti Malaysia Sabah| Pending | ## 4.4 Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------| | DD01| Outbound-only connectivity for OPDG VM, no inbound ports exposed | Approved | | DD02| Private Link not implemented due to licensing and tenant eligibility constraints | Deferred to future phase | | DD03| OPDG VM deployed with minimum 8 vCPU, 8 GB RAM, Windows Server 2019/Windows 10, .NET Framework 4.8 | Approved | | DD04| Outbound firewall rules enforced to restrict traffic to Microsoft endpoints only | Approved | # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric This section defines the roles, responsibilities, and access assignments for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) within the Microsoft Fabric platform. ## 5.1 Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that combines data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a unified SaaS experience. It enables organizations to manage data workflows, build analytics solutions, and govern access efficiently. The Fabric Administrator, also referred to as the Data Engineer, is responsible for overseeing the configuration, management, and governance of the Fabric environment. Responsibilities include: - Managing workspace creation and lifecycle - Assigning and revoking user roles and permissions - Monitoring data pipeline health and resource utilization - Ensuring compliance with Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) data governance policies - Troubleshooting and resolving operational issues Fabric Administrators possess the highest level of permissions within the workspace, enabling them to perform all administrative actions, including role assignments, resource provisioning, and security configuration. ## 5.2 Fabric-native Roles Microsoft Fabric provides several native roles to facilitate granular access control within workspaces. The primary roles are: | Permission | Admin | Contributor | Member | Viewer | |------------|-------|-------------|--------|--------| | Create, edit, delete workspace content | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | | Assign roles and permissions | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | | View workspace content | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | | Publish datasets and reports | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | | Manage workspace settings | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | - **Admin**: Full control over workspace, including management of resources and user roles. - **Contributor**: Can create and modify content but cannot manage workspace settings or assign roles. - **Member**: Can view content and participate in collaborative activities. - **Viewer**: Read-only access to workspace content. ## 5.3 UMS Workspace Role and Access Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for initial mapping and assignment of workspace roles to UMS personnel based on operational requirements. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall manage ongoing role adjustments, ensuring that access aligns with organizational changes and project needs. | No. | Workspace Role | Suggested Role | UMS PIC | |-----|---------------------|-------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Workspace Admin | Fabric Admin / Data Engineer | Dr Suraya Binti Alias

Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for maintaining and updating role assignments to reflect personnel changes and evolving project requirements. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric Design and Decision This section details the Microsoft Fabric solution architecture, workflow, access, resource organization, and governance for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform by Microsoft that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence in a single SaaS environment. It leverages OneLake as a central data lake, supports ETL pipelines, lakehouse layering, and enables seamless integration with Power BI for visualization. Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following data workflow considerations for UMS, covering the entire pipeline from data ingestion to visualization: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ![](images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: End-to-end data workflow architecture. ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following design decisions for UMS’s ETL, lakehouse layering, and ML integration: | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | ETL Pipeline Tool | Azure Data Factory and Dataflow Gen2 for orchestrating data ingestion and movement. | | 2 | Lakehouse Layering | Bronze (raw), Silver (cleansed), Gold (transformed) layers for structured data processing.| | 3 | Cleansing and Transformation | Dataflow Gen2, Notebooks, Visual Query in SQL Analytics Endpoint for Silver layer. | | 4 | ML Integration | Manual ML via Fabric Notebooks (PySpark/Python) and AutoML for model training and scoring.| | 5 | Model Registry | Use Fabric Model Registry for lifecycle management and versioning of trained models. | | 6 | Output Storage | Store scored ML outputs and performance metrics in Gold Lakehouse. | | 7 | Data Modeling | Semantic Model connects to Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake). | | 8 | Visualization | Power BI Desktop connects to Semantic Model via OneLake for reporting and analytics. | ![](images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: Machine Learning workflow within Fabric. ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions ### Conditional Access Conditional Access in Microsoft Fabric is a security feature that enables organizations to enforce access policies based on user identity, device compliance, location, and risk signals. Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS implement Conditional Access policies to safeguard sensitive data and ensure compliance. | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|--------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Conditional Access for Fabric and Power BI | Enforce policy for all privileged and data access users. | **Rationale:** Conditional Access ensures only compliant, authenticated users access critical data resources, reducing risk of unauthorized access. ### Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is a security mechanism requiring users to provide two or more verification methods before accessing resources. Enfrasys Solutions strongly recommends UMS enforce MFA for all Fabric and Power BI users. | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|-------------------------|--------------------------| | 1 | MFA Enforcement | Mandatory for all users. | **Rationale:** MFA significantly reduces the risk of account compromise and is a best practice for protecting sensitive analytics and reporting environments. ### Direct User Access Direct user access refers to users connecting to Fabric workspaces, lakehouses, and Power BI reports. Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS restrict direct access to authorized personnel only and use role-based access control (RBAC). | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|--------------------------|--------------------------| | 1 | RBAC for Workspace Access| Assign roles based on job function and least privilege principle. | **Rationale:** RBAC ensures that users have only the permissions necessary for their responsibilities, minimizing risk and maintaining data integrity. ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size The OPDG VM specification for UMS is as follows: | Component | Specification | |------------------|--------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### Management Groups Management Groups in Azure provide a logical hierarchy for organizing subscriptions and resources. Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS use Management Groups to separate environments (e.g., Production, Development) and delegate policy and access control. ### Naming and Tagging #### Azure Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |-----------------|-------------|----------------------------| | Subscription | sub | ums-prod-sub | | Resource Group | rg | ums-prod-rg | | Storage Account | st | umsprodst01 | | VM | vm | ums-prod-opdg-vm | #### Fabric Naming Convention | Item in Fabric | Naming | |-----------------|-----------------------------------------------| | Fabric Capacity | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | Workspace | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | Lakehouse | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK

Etc.. | | Semantic Model | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | Pipeline | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL
Etc.. | | Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report | #### Acronyms | Item | Acronym | |------------|----------| | Lakehouse | LK | | Dataflow | DF | | Semantic Model | SM | | Pipeline | PL | | Workspace | WS | ## 6.6 Governance Consideration ### Governance Disciplines Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for documenting the governance framework, naming standards, and resource design. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for operational adherence and cost center assignment. | Resource | Cost Center | Owner | Limit | |--------------------|--------------------|------------------------|-----------------| | Fabric Capacity | Data Analytics | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | As per budget | | Workspace | Student Affairs | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | As per budget | | VM | IT Operations | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | As per budget | ### Violation Triggers and Actions | Trigger | Action | Owner | |-----------------------------------|---------------------------------------|----------------------| | Naming convention violation | Issue remediation ticket | Enfrasys Solutions | | Unauthorized access attempt | Immediate account review and disable | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | | Resource usage exceeding limit | Alert and resource review | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | | Policy non-compliance | Governance review and corrective action| Enfrasys Solutions | # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section outlines the security architecture and controls recommended for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), focusing on network protection and encryption for all major workloads. ## 7.1 Azure Network Security Group (NSG) Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSG) are a fundamental security feature in Microsoft Azure, enabling administrators to filter network traffic to and from Azure resources. NSGs contain rules that allow or deny traffic based on source, destination, port, and protocol. They are critical for enforcing segmentation and minimizing attack surfaces. Enfrasys Solutions shall define and implement NSG rules to ensure secure connectivity for the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) and related services. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall monitor compliance and report any deviations. ### NSG Rules Table | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |--------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services| 130 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication| 140 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ![](images\slide_006_img_001.png) Figure 1: Conditional Access and NSG enforcement architecture. ## 7.2 Encryption Design Encryption is a critical component for securing data at rest and in transit within Microsoft workloads. Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and OPDG leverage Azure-native encryption mechanisms to protect sensitive information. ### Encryption at Rest - **Microsoft Fabric**: Data stored in OneLake and Fabric environments is encrypted at rest using Azure Storage Service Encryption (SSE) with AES-256. - **Power BI**: Datasets and reports are encrypted at rest using Azure-managed keys. - **OPDG**: Data cached or stored on OPDG VMs utilizes Azure Disk Encryption. ### Encryption in Transit - **Microsoft Fabric & Power BI**: All client and service communications occur over HTTPS (TLS 1.2 or higher), ensuring data is encrypted during transmission. - **OPDG**: Outbound connections to Microsoft cloud endpoints use SSL/TLS, protecting authentication and data transfer. Enfrasys Solutions shall configure all encryption settings for the above workloads. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall monitor ongoing compliance and audit encryption status. ## 7.3 Access Design (Conditional Access and MFA) Microsoft Entra ID is Microsoft's cloud-based identity and access management service, providing authentication, authorization, and security controls for Azure and Microsoft 365 resources. Conditional Access is a feature within Entra ID that allows organizations to enforce access policies based on user, location, device, and risk signals. Based on current licensing, Universiti Malaysia Sabah is not eligible for Conditional Access policies. Therefore, Enfrasys Solutions recommends enforcing Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) at the tenant level for all UMS users to mitigate unauthorized access risks. ![](images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 2: Authentication and access control workflow for Microsoft Fabric. ### Access Decisions Table | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|-----------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01| Configure Policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage | Deferred (pending license upgrade; enforce MFA at tenant level) | | DD02| Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy | Deferred (pending license upgrade; enforce MFA at tenant level) | | DD03| UMS users go through additional verification, MFA | Enforce MFA for all UMS users | | DD04| Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment | Permit access with MFA enforcement | ## 7.4 Task and Compliance Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |--------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------|----------| | NSG Rule Definition | Create and apply NSG rules for OPDG, Fabric, and Power BI connectivity | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Encryption Configuration | Configure encryption at rest and in transit for all workloads | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Compliance Monitoring | Monitor NSG, encryption, and access policy compliance; report deviations | Universiti Malaysia Sabah| Ongoing | | MFA Enforcement | Enforce MFA for all UMS users at tenant level | Enfrasys Solutions | Complete | --- # Appendix ## The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving Azure components and user access management. 

1. **Fabric-RG**: This represents a Resource Group in Azure, which is a container that holds related resources for an Azure solution. It is used to manage and organize resources effectively.

2. **UMS Fabric**: This component likely refers to a service or application running within the Azure environment, possibly related to user management or a specific business application.

3. **Conditional Access**: This is a security feature in Azure Active Directory that provides automated access control decisions for accessing cloud apps based on conditions. It ensures that only authorized users can access certain resources under specific conditions.

4. **External User**: Represents a user outside the organization who needs access to the resources or services provided by the UMS Fabric.

**Data Flow and Connections**:
- The arrow indicates a data flow or access request from the External User to the UMS Fabric.
- The Conditional Access component acts as a gatekeeper, evaluating the access request based on predefined policies before allowing the External User to interact with the UMS Fabric.
- This setup ensures secure and controlled access to resources within the Fabric-RG, leveraging Azure's security features to protect sensitive data and applications.

The image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating the authentication and access control process for Microsoft Fabric using Entra conditional access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**: The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**: The authentication request is evaluated against Entra conditional access policies.

3. **Signal Collection**: Signals are collected, which include examples such as users and groups, and network location.

4. **Decision Making**: Based on the collected signals, a decision is made regarding the access request.

5. **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, determining whether access is granted or denied.

6. **Access to Microsoft Fabric**: If access is granted, the user can interact with Microsoft Fabric and its components, including OneLake and other services depicted by various icons.

The diagram outlines a secure and conditional access workflow, ensuring that only authorized users can access Microsoft Fabric based on predefined policies.

The image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating a data processing workflow within an Azure environment, specifically using UMS Fabric and OneLake.

1. **Data Ingestion**:
   - **Source**: Data originates from the "UMS – Staging Area – Microsoft SQL Server 2017 – Data Warehouse."
   - **Process**: Data is ingested using Azure Data Factory.

2. **Data Processing Layers**:
   - **Bronze Layer**: Data is first stored in "XX_Bronze_LK Tables," representing raw or minimally processed data.
   - **Silver Layer**: Data is then transformed and stored in "XX_Silver_LK Tables," indicating a more refined dataset.
   - **Gold Layer**: Final transformations occur, and data is stored in "XX_Gold_LK Tables," ready for analysis and reporting.

3. **Data Science and Transformation**:
   - **Data Factory**: Used for orchestrating data movement and transformation.
   - **Data Science**: Involves advanced analytics and machine learning processes, likely using Azure Machine Learning or similar services.
   - **Identity_SM**: Represents a component for identity management or security, ensuring data integrity and access control.

4. **Data Storage and Access**:
   - **OneLake**: Acts as a unified data storage layer, facilitating seamless data access and integration.
   - **Power BI**: The final data visualization and reporting tool, connected via OneLake for real-time insights and analytics.

This architecture supports a robust data pipeline, enabling efficient data processing, transformation, and visualization within the Azure ecosystem.

The diagram illustrates a data flow architecture involving three main components:

1. **UMS – Staging_Area – Microsoft SQL Server 2017 – Data Warehouse**: This component represents a data warehouse using Microsoft SQL Server 2017. It serves as the staging area for data storage and processing.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**: This component acts as a bridge between the on-premises data warehouse and the cloud service. It facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports, ensuring that data can be sent out securely.

3. **UMS Fabric**: This component represents a cloud-based service or platform that interacts with the on-premises data through the OPDG. The connection is outbound and secure, indicating that data is pushed from the on-premises environment to the cloud service.

The data flow begins at the SQL Server data warehouse, which sends data to the On-Premise Data Gateway. The OPDG then securely transmits the data to the UMS Fabric, ensuring no inbound ports are needed for the connection, enhancing security.

The diagram illustrates a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure environment, integrating UMS Fabric and OneLake.

1. **Data Source**: 
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Acts as the initial data source, feeding into two potential machine learning paths.

2. **Machine Learning Paths**:
   - **AutoML**:
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Data from XX_Silver_LK Tables is processed through an automated machine learning experiment.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: The results are scored using a notebook, and the output is directed to XX_Gold_LK Tables.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modelling (Using Notebook)**: Data is manually modeled using a notebook, with results also directed to XX_Gold_LK Tables.

3. **Data Storage and Identity Management**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: Serves as the refined data storage after processing.
   - **Identity_SM**: Manages identity-related data or processes, linked with XX_Gold_LK Tables.

4. **Data Visualization**:
   - **Power BI**: Connects to XX_Gold_LK Tables via OneLake for data visualization and reporting.

This architecture supports both automated and manual machine learning processes, with a focus on flexibility and integration for data analysis and visualization.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.
